DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT REBECCA HARRINGTON'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO LIMIT OR REDUCE PLAINTIFF LEAH HICKEYS NON RETAINED EXPERTS. MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT REDUCED THE NUMBER OF NON-RETAINED EXPERTS AT TRIAL TO 8, TO BE CHOSEN BY THE PLAINTIFF AT THE END OF THE DAY TODAY. DEFENDANT MAY NOT OBJECT AT TRIAL ON THE GROUNDS THAT ANY OF THE MEDICAL RECORDS LACK AUTHENTICATION AND/OR BUSINESS RECORDS. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) MR. Notice Of Motion And Defendant Rebecca Harringtons Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Advanced From Aug-27-25 To Aug-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Defendant Rebecca Harringtons Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Michael Stephenson, Esq. 415-466-8717 Bay Area Bicycle Law PC for Plaintiff.
Sarah Pama, Esq. Mcnamara Ambacher Wheeler Hirsig & Gray LLP 707-427-3998 sarah.pama@mcnamaralaw.com for Defendant.
Court Reporter: Jane Hong Elsey, CSR 11975, 909-831-8819, csrjane@hotmail.com.

Hearing is held. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) After full arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT REBECCA HARRINGTON's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts.

Motion is granted. The Court reduced the number of non-retained experts at trial to 8, to be chosen by the Plaintiff at the end of the day today. Defendant may not object at trial on the grounds that any of the medical records lack authentication and/or business records.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Mr. Stephenson to prepare a proposed form of order. If parties cannot agree on the proposed order, both sides are to submit separate proposed orders to the court.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Jane Hong Elsey, CSR 11975, 909-831-8819, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Defendant Rebecca Harringtons Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT Rebecca Harrington's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts.

Defendant Rebecca Harrington's motion for a protective order to limit or reduce plaintiff Leah Hickey's non-retained experts is granted in part and, if there are any remaining disputes after a further meet and confer, the hearing is continued to August 25, 2025.

A protective order will issue striking all portions of Ms. Hickey's expert disclosures purporting to reserve the right to call at trial any non-retained experts not identified by name. The protective order also requires that no later than the end of the business day on August 21, 2025 Ms. Hickey must identify each named non-retained expert who she does not expect to testify at trial if Ms. Harrington stipulates to the admissibility of medical records pertaining to that expert and, as to each such expert, identify the medical records that Ms. Hickey seeks a stipulation of admissibility. The parties are required to meet and confer beginning no later than 10am on August 22, 2025 in an effort to significantly reduce the number of non-retained experts that Ms. Hickey expects to call at trial. If by 3pm on August 22, 2025 the parties have not resolved all remaining disputes regarding the number of non-retained experts that Ms. Hickey intends to call at trial, no later than 4pm on August 22, 2025 each side must send an email to department 302 stating, without argument, her position(s) as to the remaining disputes and then appear in department 302 on August 25, 2025 at 9am.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Neuropsychological Evaluation Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Michael Stephenson, Esq. 415-466-8717 Bay ARea Bicycle Law P.C. for Plaintiff.
Wilma Gray, Esq. 925-939-5330 McNamara Law for Defendant. (In-person)
Court reporter: Esther Au, CSR #14595 415-699-1341, estheraucsr@gmail.com

ARGUED. Ms. Gray contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. The Court shares its comments. Mr. Stephenson responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT REBECCA HARRINGTON's Motion To Compel Neuropsychological Evaluation Of Plaintiff.

Defendant Rebecca Harrington's motion compel neuropsychological evaluation of Plaintiff Leah Hickey.

Taken Under Submission.***

A proposed order is emailed prior to open court for the Court's review.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Esther Au, CSR #14595 415-699-1341, estheraucsr@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, an Order: Defendant Rebecca Harrington's motion to compel neuropsychological evalauation of plaintiff leah hickey is signed. (see scanned order for details)***
